title: frangula occidentalis
intro: Frangula occidentalis, also known as Cascara Sagrada, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the buckthorn family (Rhamnaceae). The plant's bark and leaves have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anthraquinone glycosides, that act as a laxative and help regulate bowel movements. The health benefits of Frangula occidentalis include the treatment of constipation, digestive issues, and even inflammation, as it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Frangula occidentalis include dried bark and leaves, which can be made into teas, capsules, or extracts, often used to support digestive health and promote regular bowel movements.
